title: Gryzzlbox

jokes.text: Forget it. It's impossible. It's not gonna work in a million years. It's pointless. I give up. / Good attitude, honey.
jokes.setup: Leslie delivers an overly dramatic defeatist rant
jokes.punchline: Ben's deadpan sarcastic response to her melodrama
jokes.explanation: Classic married couple dynamic, Ben's dry delivery contrasts perfectly with Leslie's theatrics

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: This is it. This is the end. We've lost everything. The parks department is finished. I've failed as a director, I've failed as a leader, and I've failed as a human being. We might as well just shut down the whole city and move to a deserted island because civilization is over.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Or we could just try again tomorrow.

jokes.text: Right now, my basic argument is, 'Please, give us the land. It would be so nice.'
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her strategy against a billion-dollar corporation
jokes.punchline: Her hilariously naive and simplistic approach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie characterization - her earnest optimism in an impossible situation

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, here's what we're going to do. We're going to make a website. And on that website, we're going to list all the reasons why this corporation is bad. Then we're going to email it to everyone we know. And if that doesn't work, we'll make a second website.

jokes.text: Whoa! 'Open your door'?
jokes.setup: Mysterious voice commanding them to open door
jokes.punchline: Their confused/alarmed reaction to the robotic demand
jokes.explanation: Standard surprise reaction, sets up the drone reveal

dialogue.speaker: Robotic Voice
dialogue.line: Open the door.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Did that... did that just talk to us?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I don't know. Should we open it?

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: No! Why would we open a door that's telling us to open it?

jokes.text: Oh, my God, Ben! We're about to die. The robots have come for us.
jokes.setup: A delivery drone appears
jokes.punchline: Leslie immediately assumes robot apocalypse
jokes.explanation: Leslie's tendency to catastrophize perfectly deployed for sci-fi paranoia

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my God! Is this it? Is this how it starts? The robot apocalypse? I knew it!

jokes.text: I made fun of you when you said it would happen but your novel has come true.
jokes.setup: Apparently Ben wrote a novel about robot takeover
jokes.punchline: Leslie admitting she mocked his prescient dystopian fiction
jokes.explanation: Great character detail that Ben would write dystopian sci-fi, Leslie's admission is perfectly timed

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I may have made fun of Ben's novel about robots taking over the world. But in my defense, at the time, it seemed ridiculous.

jokes.text: Okay, so they also have a squadron of flying robots giving presents to everyone. We're screwed.
jokes.setup: Learning about Gryzzl's delivery drone fleet
jokes.punchline: Leslie's matter-of-fact acceptance of defeat
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between the amazing technology and Leslie's deflated reaction

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Well, I guess that's it then. Gryzzl wins. They have drones. We don't have drones. Drones are the future. I accept this outcome.

jokes.text: It's a red and white heirloom red bean pasta, with an imported Tuscan walnut herb sauce, infused with shaved heritage Pecorino Romano. / What? You're done?
jokes.setup: Tom's elaborate description of fancy dish
jokes.punchline: Someone finished eating before he finished describing it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tom character moment - his pretentious foodie speak contrasted with practical eating

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: This is a seared foie gras terrine with a blackberry gastrique, micro greens, and a quenelle of burrata cheese, all served on a bed of—

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Done.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: —edible flowers.

jokes.text: Lucy is visiting her boyfriend Conrad in Chicago, so I'm just trying to distract myself with work so I don't think about the marathon bone sesh they're probably having.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he's cooking new dishes
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about his romantic jealousy with crude terminology
jokes.explanation: Tom's oversharing fits character but 'marathon bone sesh' is a bit juvenile

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm trying new dishes because I want to impress people. You know, show off my culinary skills.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Plus, between you and me, I get jealous when other guys cook for their girlfriends. Like, that should be my thing, you know? I should be the one making her food, making her happy...

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Look, I just don't want some other dude up in there with his spatula, okay?

jokes.text: Yeah, but all I really do is goof around all day. / Write, produce, and direct a TV show. Plus, I act in it, and I do sets, props, wardrobe. At the end, I drive everybody home.
jokes.setup: Andy dismissing his work as just goofing around
jokes.punchline: Tom listing all the actual professional work Andy does
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast showing Andy's humility vs. the reality of his substantial work

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I just goof around all day. I don't actually do anything.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You coordinate between multiple city departments, you've streamlined our permitting process, you've managed multi-million dollar budgets, and you've successfully lobbied the city council for three major initiatives.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, but like, goofing around though.

jokes.text: so I mean, a hundred bucks a week-- that's pretty fair.
jokes.setup: Andy describing all his TV show responsibilities
jokes.punchline: His conclusion that $100/week is fair compensation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - wildly undervaluing himself with innocent sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm the host, I'm the producer, I'm the director, I'm the camera operator, I'm the boom mic guy, I'm the lighting designer, I'm the editor, I'm the guy who gets the coffee... $100 a week seems fair.

jokes.text: I won't even think about Lucy and Conrad and his stupid crunches that he probably does.
jokes.setup: Tom claiming he won't think about Lucy's boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Immediately obsessing over specific details about Conrad
jokes.explanation: Good character work showing Tom's immediate contradiction and jealousy

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I'm not going to think about Lucy's boyfriend. I'm just going to move on with my life.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: But I wonder what Conrad's doing right now. Is he thinking about Lucy? Does he call her 'Luce' or 'Lucy'? I bet he calls her 'Luce.' That's so intimate. Do they have a song? They probably have a song.

jokes.text: I should probably do some crunches. Let's go! Ow! Andy, my tum tum!
jokes.setup: Tom deciding to compete with Conrad by exercising
jokes.punchline: Immediately hurting himself and using baby talk
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy elevated by Tom's childish language

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what? I'm gonna start working out. I'm gonna get huge muscles, just like Conrad.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Ow! Ow! Ow! My back! My back! Somebody call a doctor! Wahhh!

jokes.text: 'Honeybear' and 'Sugar Plum' are the special names Joe and I call each other. / Aww. / Yeah, we're adorable, but this is creepy.
jokes.setup: Donna revealing cute pet names with Joe
jokes.punchline: Her matter-of-fact acknowledgment of their adorableness before the scary part
jokes.explanation: Perfect Donna confidence - she knows they're adorable and states it as fact

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: We call each other 'Snuggle Bug' and 'Cuddle Monster.' It's very cute.

dialogue.speaker: Joe
dialogue.line: That's adorable.

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: Yeah, it is. And then he beats me.

jokes.text: Joe Biden's book, 'Biden the Rails: 1001 Poems Inspired by My Travels Through Amtrak's Northeast Corridor.'
jokes.setup: Leslie reading a book title from her Gryzzl box
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous Biden pun book title perfectly tailored to Leslie's interests
jokes.explanation: Brilliant fake book title combining Leslie's Biden obsession with terrible pun - highly quotable

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Oh my god, they sent me 'Joe Biden: A Life of Valor and Tremendous Accomplishments.' This is perfect!

jokes.text: A poster of the Supreme Court Justices sipping the Friends milkshake!
jokes.setup: Another item perfectly suited to Leslie
jokes.punchline: Bizarre mashup of government and pop culture
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Leslie-specific item combining her loves of government and friendship

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm starting a new initiative called 'Parks and Rec-reation,' where we combine municipal government with top 40 hits. Every pothole we fill gets a Beyoncé song.

jokes.text: You wanna put me on blast? I'ma put you on Front Street. / I don't understand what that means, but I think I get the context, and I love it.
jokes.setup: Donna using street slang for confrontation
jokes.punchline: Leslie's honest confusion but enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect character dynamic - Donna's street cred vs Leslie's earnest enthusiasm

dialogue.speaker: Donna
dialogue.line: You want to take this outside?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Outside? Why would we go outside? It's cold, and there's bugs. Plus, I have a really great idea for a fall festival that I want to tell you about right now.

jokes.text: Watermelon, martinis, exposed brick, Keri Russell's hair.
jokes.setup: April saying random things instead of responding normally
jokes.punchline: The weird list of positive things from her therapy
jokes.explanation: Classic April weirdness with therapeutic twist, random but specific items

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: My therapist says I should list positive things about myself. So here goes: I'm punctual, I have nice handwriting, I've never been to jail, I don't have any diseases that I know of, I'm a good listener, I'm a loyal friend, I have a good sense of humor, and I smell nice.

jokes.text: On the advice of my therapist, Dr. Richard Nygard, whenever I feel like yelling, I just take a deep breath and say three great things about being alive.
jokes.setup: April explaining her coping mechanism
jokes.punchline: April Ludgate in therapy using positive affirmations
jokes.explanation: Hilarious character development - April using therapy techniques is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm going to say something positive about myself. I'm April Ludgate, and I'm a talented, intelligent, and valuable member of society.

dialogue.speaker: Therapist
dialogue.line: That's wonderful, April. How does that make you feel?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Like I'm lying to myself. But in a healthy way.

jokes.text: I started here when I was 20, and now I'm old and gross and directionless. Those kids are me ten years ago, and this internship is the videotape from The Ring.
jokes.setup: April reflecting on her career path
jokes.punchline: Comparing the internship to a cursed horror movie artifact
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor combining April's darkness with horror movie reference, excellent structure

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about my time as an intern. It was like being cursed by a horror movie artifact. Every day I thought it would kill me, but it just made me stronger and more bitter.

jokes.text: It's too late for me. I've seen too much. But maybe I can save them. / You're all gonna die in here!
jokes.setup: April's noble sacrifice narrative
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting herself by scaring the interns
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - wanting to save them by traumatizing them first

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I've decided to take the interns under my wing. I'm going to be a mentor figure, guide them, help them grow.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: BOO! Get out of here! This is my office now!

jokes.text: They sent my husband three boxes of genital cream to his office. My husband's splotchy genitals are no one's business, except for his, mine, and his many genital doctors.
jokes.setup: Woman complaining about privacy invasion
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about husband's medical condition in graphic detail
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe humor, relies on medical gross-out but has good escalation

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: I can't believe you people are invading my privacy like this. It's outrageous!

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: My husband has been suffering from a severe case of hemorrhoids for three weeks now. The bleeding is just uncontrollable, and the itching is so intense he can barely sit down. His doctor says it might require surgery.

jokes.text: This is actually Ham-uel L. Jackson from the movie, Pork Fiction, it's extremely rare, and I am keeping it.
jokes.setup: Man explaining his secret hobby of collecting movie star pigs
jokes.punchline: Elaborate puns combining Samuel L. Jackson with pig theme
jokes.explanation: Creative pun work with good specificity, both character name and movie title punned

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: I collect pigs that look like famous movie stars. My prize pig looks just like Samuel L. Jackson.

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: I call him Samuel L. Pigson.

jokes.text: But still, they no right to give me something I will treasure the rest of my life.
jokes.setup: Man complaining about privacy invasion
jokes.punchline: Admitting he loves the gift while protesting it
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction showing the conflict between principle and desire

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: I can't believe you people are invading my privacy like this. This is a violation of my constitutional rights.

dialogue.speaker: Citizen
dialogue.line: But I have to say, this gift basket is amazing. These are my favorite cookies.

jokes.text: I opened my box in front of all of my friends, and it was a bunch of Virginia Woolf novels. Now Miley and Haley know I like to read. What if they tell Evan?
jokes.setup: Young person complaining about privacy
jokes.punchline: Being ashamed that friends know they read literature
jokes.explanation: Good social commentary on youth culture where intellectualism is stigmatized

dialogue.speaker: Young citizen
dialogue.line: I'm really concerned about my privacy online. I don't want people knowing my personal information.

dialogue.speaker: Young citizen
dialogue.line: Like, I'd be mortified if my friends found out I read literature.

jokes.text: Actually, this is Tom Sell-oink, but, you know, close enough. / Actually, I'm gonna need that back.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to demonstrate with the pig
jokes.punchline: Man immediately reclaiming his precious collectible
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to establish how precious the collectibles are

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Look, it's not that hard. You just need to show the pig some affection and—

dialogue.speaker: Pig collector
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! That's my pig!

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I'm just demonstrating—

dialogue.speaker: Pig collector
dialogue.line: Nobody touches Mr. Truffles but me!

jokes.text: It includes such hits as: 'First Trip to the Dentist,' 'Sonia Tries Pizza,' and 'Waterpark Vomit Chain Reaction.'
jokes.setup: Ben describing his family photo album
jokes.punchline: List escalating from cute to gross with clinical precision
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and Ben's matter-of-fact delivery makes the gross-out funny

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Here's me at age five with my first dog. Here's me at seven with a scraped knee. Here's me at nine covered in mud. Here's me at eleven with a rash that the dermatologist said was 'concerning.' Here's me at thirteen with what I'm pretty sure was a minor infection. Here's me at fifteen covered in what I can only describe as a biological hazard.

jokes.text: What, I'm just gonna carry around pictures of my child where anyone could see them?
jokes.setup: Normal practice of showing child photos
jokes.punchline: Ron treating it like a security breach
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron paranoia applied to mundane parenting situation

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Why would you show me a photo of your child? That's a security risk. Now I know what they look like. If they ever go missing, I could identify them immediately.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You've essentially given me a dossier. I could find them anywhere.

jokes.text: and they do seem to, as they say, 'keep it 100.'
jokes.setup: Ron talking about Gryzzl lawyers
jokes.punchline: Ron awkwardly using modern slang
jokes.explanation: Ron trying to use contemporary slang is inherently funny, perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Those Gryzzl lawyers are relentless. They've got me in a corner.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I guess you could say... they're really 'slaying' me right now. No cap, fam. That's what the kids say, right?

jokes.text: Yes, hello, I'm Darlene Johannsen, and this is my assistant-turned-lover, Gregory Strong. Ours is a new romance, but one that sent shockwaves through my architecture firm.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ben going undercover
jokes.punchline: Leslie's elaborate fake backstory with unnecessary romantic details
jokes.explanation: Leslie's overelaborate cover story is very characteristic but slightly long

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Okay, so my name is Brenda Withers, I'm a dental hygienist from Muncie, Indiana. I have two cats named Mr. Whiskers and Princess Fluffington. I'm divorced, but it was amicable. My ex-husband Brad and I still text on birthdays. He's remarried now to a woman named Tiffany, and honestly, I'm happy for them. They have a golden retriever named Biscuit.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Leslie, why would any of that matter for going undercover?

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Because details make it real, Ben. People trust specificity.

jokes.text: Okay, that doesn't help me.
jokes.setup: Intern giving normal answer about loving parks
jokes.punchline: April's blunt dismissal of enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical April response, dismissive but not cruel

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I hate it. Parks are just where people go to die.

jokes.text: The only thing I love more than parks is recreation. / Really? / No, I just needed college credit.
jokes.setup: Seemingly passionate statement about parks and rec
jokes.punchline: Immediate deflation revealing it's just academic requirement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with clean structure and realistic cynicism

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Parks and Recreation is the most important field of study. It shapes communities, creates opportunities for public engagement, and fundamentally transforms how we experience our shared spaces.

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: It's literally the only reason I'm here.

jokes.text: Well, my mom's the devil and my dad's a dumb doctor.
jokes.setup: April asking about parents
jokes.punchline: Casual description of parents as devil and idiot
jokes.explanation: Standard edgy teen humor but delivered naturally

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: What are your parents like?

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Oh, my mom's a devil and my dad's an idiot.

jokes.text: I feel like you have almost zero potential. I think that's cool.
jokes.setup: April assessing intern Jen
jokes.punchline: Finding lack of potential appealing rather than concerning
jokes.explanation: Perfect April logic - appreciating failure as authentic and relatable

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Jen has no skills, no experience, and no discernible future. It's actually kind of appealing.

jokes.text: I love being Andy's agent. He does all the work, and I get 10% just for showing up. It's like a chef at a restaurant, making all the food, and then the owner gets a profit-- which is my other job!
jokes.setup: Tom explaining how easy being an agent is
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's describing his own business model where he exploits others
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Tom accidentally describes his own exploitation

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Being an agent is so easy. You just take a percentage of everything your clients make, and you don't have to do any work.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Wait... that's my business model.

jokes.text: in the words of Jerry Maguire-- / 'The human head weighs 8 pounds.' / No. 'Show me the money.'
jokes.setup: Tom setting up famous movie quote
jokes.punchline: Andy knowing obscure trivia from the movie instead of the famous line
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - missing the obvious cultural reference for random detail

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Okay, so you know that famous line from Casablanca? 'Here's looking at you, kid.'

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Oh yeah, that's the one where Rick says it to Ilsa at the end, right? Actually, did you know that Humphrey Bogart was only 25 years older than Ingrid Bergman? And the studio was worried about their age difference!

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: ...I was just trying to quote the movie.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Also, Bogart's character was originally supposed to die in the scene, but they changed it because test audiences wanted a happier ending!

jokes.text: You discovered Selena Gomez? / Different Selena Gomez.
jokes.setup: Manager claiming to have discovered celebrity
jokes.punchline: Admitting it's a different person with the same name
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective deflation of manager's boast with economical delivery

dialogue.speaker: Station Manager
dialogue.line: I discovered a celebrity! It's Ben Wyatt!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, really? THE Ben Wyatt?

dialogue.speaker: Station Manager
dialogue.line: Well, no. It's a different Ben Wyatt. He works at a shoe store.

jokes.text: What did you want to be when you were little? / A scary mermaid that lures sailors to their death.
jokes.setup: Standard childhood aspiration question
jokes.punchline: Extremely specific and dark fantasy career
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of innocent question with very specific dark fantasy

jokes.text: Okay, side note, I'm gonna file papers to adopt you as my child, so keep an eye out for that.
jokes.setup: April impressed by Jen's dark response
jokes.punchline: Immediate decision to legally adopt fellow cynical teen
jokes.explanation: Perfect April absurdism - making major life decisions based on one good answer

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I'm adopting you. I've already filled out the paperwork.

dialogue.speaker: Jen
dialogue.line: Really?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: No, but I'm going to.

jokes.text: Go do something fun like trying to control birds with your mind or posting internet comments as Michael Jackson's ghost.
jokes.setup: April suggesting alternatives to internship
jokes.punchline: Two completely random and bizarre activities presented as normal options
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random April suggestions, both specific and absurd in different ways

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: If you don't want to do the internship, you could always become a professional snake charmer or learn to forge medieval weapons.

jokes.text: As you know, the cameras on your phones are always on, whether you're using them or not. / I'm sorry, they are?
jokes.setup: Gryzzl casually revealing surveillance
jokes.punchline: Leslie's shocked realization this isn't common knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good tech paranoia comedy, Leslie's genuine shock sells the creepiness

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl employee
dialogue.line: We track everything. Your location, your purchases, your search history, your conversations.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Wait, wait, wait. You're just... casually mentioning this?

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl employee
dialogue.line: Of course. It's in the terms and conditions.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Nobody reads those!

dialogue.speaker: Gryzzl employee
dialogue.line: We know.

jokes.text: to when you ladies need to score some new 'pons.
jokes.setup: Roscoe listing data they collect
jokes.punchline: Awkward bro trying to casually reference feminine hygiene products
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from awkward tech bro trying to sound casual about periods

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: We collect data on everything - shopping habits, browsing history, pharmaceutical purchases...

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: You know, tampons, pads, that sort of thing.

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: It's just... data.

jokes.text: I mean, we just wanna learn everything about everyone and track them wherever they go and anticipate what they're about to do.
jokes.setup: Roscoe claiming Gryzzl isn't scary
jokes.punchline: Immediately describing completely dystopian surveillance state
jokes.explanation: Good irony - reassuring tone while describing terrifying reality

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: Gryzzl isn't scary. They're just a company that wants to help people.

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: Sure, they monitor every purchase you make, track your location 24/7, predict your behavior before you know what you're going to do, and have access to all your personal data. But that's not scary, that's just business.

jokes.text: TV. Retro. I love it.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggesting they debate on TV
jokes.punchline: Roscoe treating television as charmingly old-fashioned
jokes.explanation: Minor tech culture joke about traditional media being outdated

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: We should do a debate on television.

dialogue.speaker: Roscoe
dialogue.line: Television? That's adorable. You still think people watch television.

jokes.text: Tonight, on The Perdples Court, we will have the guests that have been booked for tonight's show-- the plaintiff, Leslie Knope, who has some big allegations against Gryzzl.
jokes.setup: Perd introducing the show
jokes.punchline: Redundantly explaining he'll have the guests that were booked
jokes.explanation: Classic Perd redundancy, true to character but not exceptional

dialogue.speaker: Perd Hapley
dialogue.line: I'm Perd Hapley, and I have some news. On today's show, we'll be talking to the guests we've booked to be on the show.

jokes.text: December 18, 2015. That was the opening day of Star Wars: Episode VII. You were muttering that date in your sleep for months.
jokes.setup: Ron pointing out when Leslie missed the contract change
jokes.punchline: Leslie's Star Wars obsession was so intense Ron heard her sleep-talking
jokes.explanation: Great character detail showing Leslie's obsessive nature through Ron's observation

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: You missed the contract change on page forty-seven.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: I was up until 3 AM watching the original trilogy again.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I heard you sleep-talking last night. You kept saying 'Help me, Obi-Wan Kenobi. You're my only hope.'

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's... that's actually beautiful.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: It was disturbing.

jokes.text: Is Star Wars the one with the little wizard boy?
jokes.setup: Context of Star Wars discussion
jokes.punchline: Ron confusing Star Wars with Harry Potter
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron pop culture ignorance, mixing up major franchises

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't understand the appeal. A bunch of wizards waving sticks around in space?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: That's not a story, that's a weapon malfunction.

jokes.text: Well, invent another character. Uh, here you go: Kung-Fu Billy. Jeremy Punch-Guy. Ed Kick.
jokes.setup: Manager telling Andy to create new character
jokes.punchline: Increasingly lazy and generic martial arts character names
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing manager's lazy creativity and disrespect for Andy's work

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Andy, I need you to create a new character for the show.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, how about... Master Dragon?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: That's too specific. Something more generic.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Alright... Sensei Strike?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Still too creative.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, uh... Karate Guy?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: You're getting there.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: How about just... Punch?

dialogue.speaker: Jerry
dialogue.line: Perfect.

jokes.text: I once sat in the original Batmobile.
jokes.setup: Manager listing his impressive achievements
jokes.punchline: Including sitting in a car as a major accomplishment
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing manager's inflated sense of importance

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I've accomplished many things in my life. I've built a canoe from scratch. I've fished for sturgeon. I've negotiated with drug lords.

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: And I once sat in a car for three days straight without moving or speaking to anyone.

jokes.text: That's why the only contract I've ever signed is my Mulligan's Steakhouse club card. And even then, I used a fake name. Les. Les Vegetables.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining his contract aversion
jokes.punchline: Using an obviously fake name that's anti-Ron (vegetables)
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron moment - paranoid about steakhouse loyalty cards, fake name is perfectly anti-Ron

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I don't believe in contracts. They're a sign of distrust between people.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: If I ever had to sign a contract, I'd use a fake name. Something like... Turnip Slamdunk.

jokes.text: Recently, I've been feeling like I've wasted the last ten years of my life, and it all started with this internship
jokes.setup: April explaining her career advice
jokes.punchline: Blaming her success and growth on the internship as if it ruined her life
jokes.explanation: Good April character work - viewing her positive development as somehow negative

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I owe all my success and personal growth to this internship. It completely ruined my life.

jokes.text: Sweet potato pie, unlikely animal friend pairings, Jennifer Love Hewitt.
jokes.setup: April getting emotional during conversation
jokes.punchline: Using her therapy technique with three random positive things
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier therapy technique, shows April actually using it

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Okay, fine. I'm going to use my therapy technique. Three positive things: One, I have a job. Two, I have friends. Three, I'm not dead.

jokes.text: Well, Perd-- / It's 'Judge Perd.'
jokes.setup: Leslie addressing Perd normally
jokes.punchline: Perd insisting on his temporary judicial title
jokes.explanation: Standard Perd pompousness about his hosting role

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You can't keep introducing yourself as Judge Perd Hapley.

dialogue.speaker: Perd
dialogue.line: But I was a judge for one day.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You were a traffic court judge for four hours.

dialogue.speaker: Perd
dialogue.line: Judge Perd Hapley.

jokes.text: To be honest, Judge Perd is stumped by this case. I've also misplaced my judge hammer.
jokes.setup: Perd trying to render verdict
jokes.punchline: Admitting incompetence and calling gavel a 'judge hammer'
jokes.explanation: Good Perd moment combining incompetence with wrong terminology

dialogue.speaker: Perd
dialogue.line: I have to be honest with you, I have no idea what's going on here. I don't understand the case, I don't understand the evidence, and frankly, I'm not even sure what this judge hammer does.

jokes.text: Therefore, I must declare a mistrial, which is a term I've heard people use in the movies. Tap, tap, tap. Case ended.
jokes.setup: Perd trying to sound official
jokes.punchline: Admitting he doesn't know legal terms and making gavel sounds
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Perd's incompetence with childish gavel sounds

dialogue.speaker: Perd
dialogue.line: As a journalist, I must say that this is a clear violation of... uh... the legal thing. The statute. The... *makes gavel sound* ...and that's the law.

jokes.text: Get this--they're gonna pay me the minimum wage.
jokes.setup: Andy announcing good news about his new job
jokes.punchline: Being excited about minimum wage as if it's generous
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy enthusiasm for objectively terrible deal

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I got the job! I'm gonna be making minimum wage!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's like, what, a thousand dollars a year? I'm rich!

jokes.text: If there's something that you want badly, you just have to believe that it's gonna work out. / That's actually really good advice for anything.
jokes.setup: Tom giving Andy life advice
jokes.punchline: Andy finding profound wisdom in generic positive thinking
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andy's ability to find meaning in simple statements

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: You know what your problem is, Andy? You don't believe in yourself enough.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Really? I think I believe in myself a lot.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: No, you don't. You need to wake up every morning, look yourself in the mirror, and say 'I am going to have an amazing day.'

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wow. That's... that's actually really good advice, Tom.

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: I know.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Where do you come up with this stuff?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Fortune cookies.

jokes.text: Victor Garber, James Garner, Jennifer Garner. I go alphabetical now.
jokes.setup: April using her therapy technique again
jokes.punchline: Revealing she's systematized it by alphabet
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing April's systematic approach to emotional management

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I've systematized my therapy technique by alphabet. A is for avoidance, B is for bottling things up, C is for compartmentalization, D is for denial, E is for excessive sarcasm...

jokes.text: But what I would like to do is have a glass of wine and talk about that shade Nicki Minaj threw at Jesse Eisenberg at the BAFTA awards. / Mm. He deserved it. / No. he didn't!
jokes.setup: Lucy wanting to discuss celebrity gossip
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately having strong opinions about obscure celebrity drama
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tom's pop culture obsession and strong opinions

dialogue.speaker: Lucy
dialogue.line: Oh my god, did you see what happened with those celebrities?

dialogue.speaker: Tom
dialogue.line: Which ones? Because I have VERY strong opinions about the situation with that one actor's third cousin's feuds with reality TV stars from 2009.

jokes.text: This is a flying robot I just shot out of the sky after it delivered a package to my house.
jokes.setup: Ron arriving with mysterious object
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to destroying delivery drone
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response to modern technology - immediate violence delivered matter-of-factly. Excellent escalation.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I may have destroyed a delivery drone with my bare hands.

jokes.text: No one is safe from these bastards. Tell me what to do, Leslie. I wanna help you take them down.
jokes.setup: Ron's realization about Gryzzl's reach
jokes.punchline: Ron Swanson asking for orders and wanting to fight corporations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Ron's libertarian principles flip when his family is threatened, perfectly executed

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about Gryzzl, and I realize they have tentacles in every aspect of our lives. They know where we live, where we work, where we shop, what we like, what we don't like. They know everything about us.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: So I've decided to take them on. I'm going to fight Gryzzl with everything I have.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: First, I'm going to need some supplies. Do you have any orders for me?

tmdb_overview: Leslie and Ben suspect that Gryzzl is invading the privacy of Pawnee residents. April addresses the new Parks and Rec interns, while Tom helps Andy renegotiate a contract.
insight: Gryzzlbox's escalation comedy hits 82.6 with 60 jokes in 60 minutes—consistent density masks zero standout moments.
